question: A boy complains of bleeding gums, swollen, Joints with hemorrhage into joints. His paternal and maternal uncle complains of same problem. It is due to deficiency of factor:

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: This presentation suggests hemophilia, which typically presents as spontaneous or minor injury-induced joint bleeds along with gum bleeding. Hemophilia B (factor IX deficiency) has a higher prevalence among males and can be inherited from either parent through an affected male relative, fitting this family history pattern. Factor VIII deficiency would more commonly present without the specific family history noted here. Therefore,
Answer: B
